figures.caption: Figure 3: Bouncing balls: prediction error vs. wall-clock time. The horizontal axis exposes the throughput differential: the JAX-AD curve reaches the same accuracy plateau as the PyTorch-AD curve in 5× to 35× less wall-clock time across the three problem sizes.

Table 2: Multi-ball bouncing system with N ∈ {3, 7, 15} balls. Per-parameter relative error in %, final validation loss, and total wall-clock time. Budget: 300 iters for N ∈ {3, 7} and 600 iters for N = 15. Times measured on a single CPU thread.
Figure 2: Cauer ladder: Adam loss histories on log scale. The AD curve descends ∼5 decades whereas the DA curve plateaus at ∼ 10−4; the gap is consistent across the last ∼ 100 iterations.
Table 3: Comparison of differentiable event-learning approaches.
